# flashduty_escalate_rule (Resource)

Manages a Flashduty escalation rule for a channel.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create a simple escalation rule
resource "flashduty_escalate_rule" "default" {
  channel_id  = 6148622168454
  rule_name   = "Default Escalation"
  description = "Default escalation policy for all alerts"
  template_id = "6321aad26c12104586a88916"

  layers = [
    {
      escalate_window = 15
      target = {
        person_ids = [75340551232454]
        by = {
          follow_preference = true
          critical          = ["email", "sms", "voice"]
          warning           = ["email", "sms"]
          info              = ["email"]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      escalate_window = 30
      target = {
        schedule_ids = [4973305527804]
        by = {
          follow_preference = true
          critical          = ["email", "sms", "voice"]
          warning           = ["email", "sms"]
          info              = ["email"]
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}

# Create an escalation rule with full configuration
resource "flashduty_escalate_rule" "critical" {
  channel_id  = 6148622168454
  rule_name   = "Critical Alerts Escalation"
  description = "Escalation policy for critical alerts"
  template_id = "6321aad26c12104586a88916"
  aggr_window = 60

  layers = [
    {
      max_times       = 3
      notify_step     = 10
      escalate_window = 15
      force_escalate  = true
      target = {
        person_ids = [75340551232454]
        by = {
          follow_preference = true
          critical          = ["email", "sms", "voice"]
          warning           = ["email", "sms"]
          info              = ["email"]
        }
        webhooks = [
          {
            type     = "wecom"
            settings = jsonencode({ token = "https://qyapi.weixin.qq.com/xxx" })
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]

  # Only apply during weekday business hours
  time_filters = [
    {
      start  = "09:00"
      end    = "18:00"
      repeat = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    }
  ]

  # Only match critical severity alerts
  filters = [
    {
      conditions = [
        {
          key  = "severity"
          oper = "IN"
          vals = ["Critical"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `channel_id` (Number) The ID of the channel this rule belongs to.
- `layers` (Attributes List) The escalation layers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers))
- `rule_name` (String) The name of the escalation rule.
- `template_id` (String) The notification template ID.

### Optional

- `aggr_window` (Number) Aggregation window in seconds (0-3600).
- `description` (String) The description of the rule.
- `filters` (Attributes List) Alert matching filter conditions (OR between groups, AND within conditions). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--filters))
- `priority` (Number) The priority of the escalation rule for ordering.
- `time_filters` (Attributes List) Time-based filter conditions for when this rule applies. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--time_filters))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the escalate rule.

<a id="nestedatt--layers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers`

Required:

- `target` (Attributes) The escalation target. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers--target))

Optional:

- `escalate_window` (Number) Minutes before escalating to next layer. Defaults to 30.
- `force_escalate` (Boolean) Whether to escalate even after acknowledgment.
- `max_times` (Number) Maximum notification times. Defaults to 2.
- `notify_step` (Number) Notification interval in minutes. Defaults to 10.

<a id="nestedatt--layers--target"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers.target`

Optional:

- `by` (Attributes) Notification preference settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers--target--by))
- `emails` (List of String) List of email addresses for dispatch.
- `person_ids` (List of Number) List of person IDs.
- `schedule_ids` (List of Number) List of schedule IDs. Maps to `schedule_to_role_ids` in API with default role `[0]`.
- `team_ids` (List of Number) List of team IDs.
- `webhooks` (Attributes List) Webhook notification targets. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--layers--target--webhooks))

<a id="nestedatt--layers--target--by"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers.target.by`

Optional:

- `critical` (List of String) Notification channels for Critical severity (e.g., `email`, `sms`, `phone`, `im`).
- `follow_preference` (Boolean) Whether to follow user's notification preferences.
- `info` (List of String) Notification channels for Info severity.
- `warning` (List of String) Notification channels for Warning severity.


<a id="nestedatt--layers--target--webhooks"></a>
### Nested Schema for `layers.target.webhooks`

Required:

- `settings` (String) Webhook settings as JSON string.
- `type` (String) Webhook type (e.g., `feishu_app`, `wecom`, `dingtalk`, `slack`).




<a id="nestedatt--filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filters`

Required:

- `conditions` (Attributes List) Filter conditions (AND logic). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--filters--conditions))

<a id="nestedatt--filters--conditions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filters.conditions`

Required:

- `key` (String) The attribute key to filter on (e.g., `title`, `severity`, `labels.xxx`).
- `oper` (String) The operator (`IN`, `NOTIN`).
- `vals` (List of String) The values to match.



<a id="nestedatt--time_filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `time_filters`

Optional:

- `cal_id` (String) Calendar ID for calendar-based filtering.
- `end` (String) End time in HH:MM format.
- `is_off` (Boolean) Whether to match off-duty time from the calendar.
- `repeat` (List of Number) Days of the week (0=Sunday, 1=Monday, ..., 6=Saturday).
- `start` (String) Start time in HH:MM format.
